_about: Endpoint field-quirk map for sprite generation through `vg generate run <endpoint_id> --<field> <value>`. Different endpoints name the same concept differently (image_url vs image_urls vs start_image_url) and only some support a first+last-frame edit. This map saves the agent from re-deriving each schema. It is NOT a guarantee of availability or current defaults: confirm with `vg generate schema <endpoint_id> --json` before relying on a field, and `vg generate models <query> --json` to discover endpoints. Endpoint IDs are passed to the CLI verbatim — never rewrite them.

_usage.image: vg generate run <endpoint_id> --prompt "..." [--image_urls '["<url>"]'] --aspect_ratio 1:1 --resolution 1K --download out.png --json   (edit endpoints take --image_urls, a JSON array; t2i endpoints omit it)
_usage.video: vg generate run <endpoint_id> --prompt "..." --<inputImageField> "<url>" [--<endImageField> "<url>"] --duration <n> --download clip.mp4 --json   (async by default; poll with `vg generate status <endpoint_id> <request_id> --result --download clip.mp4 --json`)
_usage.queue: Most generation endpoints are queue-based — fire with --async, poll with `vg generate status`. Background removal (bria) is synchronous: run it without --async.

defaults._motionRationale: Short i2v clips (duration 1-2s) are the recommended default for sprite motion: a short sample gives the model less time to drift identity/palette/scale. Prefer several short attempts over one long cinematic clip. Use chroma key + despill (chroma_clean.py) or bria to remove the matte after frame recovery.

nano-banana-pro-t2i.note: Recommended default for character/anchor generation: fast, strong on chunky pixel art.

nano-banana-pro-edit.note: Edit endpoint: derive directional anchors / variants from an approved character image. The i2v motion clip is generated from the resulting anchor.

gpt-image-2-t2i.note: Stronger character consistency + text rendering; slower and pricier than nano-banana-pro.

bria-background-remove.note: Semantic segmentation (not chroma key) — works even on white-on-white. Synchronous: do not pass --async. Alternative to chroma_clean.py when you did not generate on a flat matte.

grok-imagine-video-i2v.note: Recommended sprite-motion default. duration=1 (use 2 for walk so a full stride lands). No negative_prompt field — steer with POSITIVE descriptions of the wanted pose, not 'do not' phrasing.

seedance-2.0-i2v.note: Supports a last frame (end_image_url) — good for transitions like get_up (start=downed frame, end=standing anchor). Min duration 4s.

wan-2.7-i2v.note: Higher fidelity, supports end frame + negative_prompt. Min duration 2s; expect an early settling segment — start frame review near the middle of the clip.

kling-v3-pro-i2v.note: Note the input field is start_image_url (not image_url).
